_meta.title: Texting Wrapped — Source Registry
_meta.description: Canonical source list keyed by source_key used across benchmarks.json and age_rubric.json. Use for in-product citations and confidence display.

stach_2024.name: Stach et al. 2024, Sensors (Basel)
stach_2024.detail: 9.9M Android notifications, 537 users w/ demographics; in-the-wild 2018-2020; Germany/Europe, 84% male

pew_2011.name: Pew Research Center 2011 — How Americans Use Text Messaging
pew_2011.detail: Nationally representative US sample; Aaron Smith

hinge_2024.name: Hinge 2024 Gen Z Report
hinge_2024.detail: n=15,000 Hinge users globally, surveyed Aug 2023; dating context

messagedesk_2026.name: MessageDesk 2026 text messaging stats
messagedesk_2026.detail: No named primary source for the unread figures

seufert_2016.name: Seufert et al. 2016, IFIP Networking — WhatsApp group communication

nngroup_participation.name: Nielsen Norman Group — Participation Inequality (Jakob Nielsen 2006)

statista_2023.name: Statista Dec. 2023 — US adults emoji usage frequency by age

adobe_2022.name: Adobe 2022 U.S. Emoji Trend Report
adobe_2022.detail: n=5,000 US frequent emoji users (not representative of all adults)

cjlls_emoji.name: Canadian Journal of Language and Linguistics — generational emoji study
cjlls_emoji.detail: Boomers use emoji significantly less than Gen Z (d=-0.645, p<0.001); no sig. Gen Z vs Millennial difference (p=0.337)

meta_2015.name: Meta Research 2015 — The Not-So-Universal Language of Laughter
meta_2015.detail: De-identified Facebook posts, last week May 2015; haha 51.4%, emoji 33.7%, hehe 12.7%, lol 1.9%; lol & hehe skew OLDEST

gunraj_2016.name: Gunraj et al. 2016, Computers in Human Behavior
gunraj_2016.detail: Binghamton; n=126 undergrads; period-ending texts rated less sincere (digital medium only)

binghamton_2025.name: Binghamton 2025 follow-up (Klin et al., Frontiers in Psychology)

conversation_2016.name: The Conversation 2016 — why period in text sounds insincere
conversation_2016.detail: Cites Baron & Ling; Tannen on exclamation as sincerity

cgk_2025.name: Center for Generational Kinetics / Jason Dorsey via Reader's Digest 2025

ucla_2024.name: UCLA Languaged Life 2024 — generational social media communication
ucla_2024.detail: Gen X ~67 words/post vs fewer for Gen Z; small study, social posts not private texts

mcculloch_2019.name: Gretchen McCulloch — Because Internet (2019)
mcculloch_2019.detail: Internet-socialization cohorts; CAPS, bursts, Old/Full/Post-Internet People

dictionary_2022.name: Dictionary.com 2022 — Gen Z explains emoji to Millennials

yougov_atlassian.name: YouGov/Atlassian survey of 10,000 employees
yougov_atlassian.detail: US/FR/DE/IN/AU; Gen Z reads 👍 as passive-aggressive

herring_emoji.name: Herring & Zhukova, Indiana University — Emoji, Gender and Age
herring_emoji.detail: n=519, ages 18-70+; older users interpret emoji literally/confusingly

apa_2026.name: APA Monitor 2026 — psychology of slang, identity & belonging

kineticmc_2021.name: KineticMC 2021 — you are only as old as your texting style
